assessment_items.question_content: **This ship burial was found on a farm in [[☃ dropdown 1]] and was filled with [[☃ dropdown 2]].**
hints: **This ship burial was found on a farm in East Anglia in England and was filled with treasure.**

assessment_items.question_content:
**The objects from the ship burial reflect a time of . . .**

[[☃ radio 1]]
hints: **The objects from the ship burial reflect a time of political unrest and Christianization in England.**

assessment_items.question_content: **So many objects, including the body of the deceased man, have rotted over time. The only things that survived were made of [[☃ dropdown 1]].**
hints: **So many objects, including the body of the deceased man, have rotted over time. The only things that survived were made of metal. None of the original timber survived from the ship, but the form of the ship was perfectly preserved.**

assessment_items.question_content:
**Many of the objects in the burial are associated with cooking and eating, such as pots and drinking vessels.**

[[☃ radio 1]]
hints: **Many of the objects in the burial are associated with warfare, such as helmets and shields.**

assessment_items.question_content:
**What characteristics of the early medieval period are suggested by the purse cover from Sutton Hoo?**

![](${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/797def937244b194582e2fbbf0b6ad57.jpg)

[[☃ radio 1]]
hints: **Just like today, people in the early medieval period adorned themselves with objects made of precious materials to show their social status and wealth.**
**The intricacy of the design and metalworking techniques reflects the high level of skill of medieval craftsmen.**

**While we cannot be sure of the meaning of the animal forms used to decorate this object, their use *does not* suggest a prohibition on religious imagery in the early medieval period. All the other answers are correct except for this one.** 
 
**[Read this essay to review](/humanities/medieval-world/latin-western-europe/early-medieval/a/the-sutton-hoo-purse-lid)**

choices.content: Individuals used luxury objects as status symbols.
Economic and cultural exchange occurred throughout Western Europe.
Skilled craftsman produced intricately designed decorative arts.
Prohibitions existed to restrict the use of religious imagery.
Animal forms provided decorative effect and symbolic value.

assessment_items.question_content:
**Compare these objects from the burial at Sutton Hoo.  What attributes of early medieval art in Western Europe do they share?** 

![](${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/6a0caaaa549a154932c71483f5d9c585.jpg) 
Purse Cover from Sutton Hoo

![](${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/6b1534e02d28565f9187fdf0897f4da6.jpg)
Reconstructed Helmet from Sutton Hoo

[[☃ radio 1]]
hints: **While the helmet includes narrative scenes of warriors in addition to fantastical animals, the purse cover's stylized animal forms may reference power and strength.**

**All of these characteristics found in early medieval art of Western Europe are demonstrated by these examples.**  

**[Read this essay for information about the purse cover from Sutton Hoo.](/humanities/medieval-world/latin-western-europe/early-medieval/a/the-sutton-hoo-purse-lid)**

**[Read this essay for information about the helmet from Sutton Hoo.](/humanities/medieval-world/latin-western-europe/early-medieval/a/the-sutton-hoo-helmet)**

choices.content: The use of stylized animal forms.
The combination of precious materials for decorative effect.
The emphasis on patterns and intricate linear detail.
The symbolic reference to strength and valor in battle.
The use of art objects for personal adornment.
